methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $626M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Poolside is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant that has raised $626M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 9 private firms at the same stage, including Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M), plus one public reference point (Palantir).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.2B. The peer-median method anchors to the five-company median valuation of $5.0B within the same sector and stage cohort. The peer range spans $500M to $32.0B, reflecting material heterogeneity within frontier AI at this funding stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.0B (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median estimate of $3.1B. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty around Poolside's relative competitive positioning and revenue traction versus peers like Physical Intelligence and Safe Superintelligence. Capital efficiency and path to material revenue generation are the primary value drivers differentiating firms across this comp set.
